title: What is React
description: A Breif General Overview Of React

body.raw:
![Pinterest art from art station](https://i.pinimg.com/564x/c3/e3/a6/c3e3a6fcbbc3432c24913d5963459497.jpg) ##### Arts By Synthetic Heartbeats - what is react ? - why use it ? - is it even worth learning it in 2023? 👊 We'll just take a brief overview of what React is. Just Enought to get you curious and start off digging on your own. ⚖ I'll be assumimg that you have a general understandidng of the basic web technologies: - HMTL 5 - CSS - JS ### So! What is React? It is a javascript library that makes it easy to build user interfaces. Let me explain 😎 > 🔖 Library > > Someone elses code made available for you to use. usually containing functions > that already do the things you'd manually not want to implement. or maintain so what are this things you'd want to do UI wise? 🤔 - Build a UI. - Compose Dynamic layouts. - Modularize your Code for re-use. - Easily update UI elements etc. #### But all these can be done using vanilla Js. **Fair Point. But it can really get hairy and hard to maintain when you start putting together something a bit more complex. for small sized applications and practice this is no issue.It all comes down to managing the codebase. i.e easily making changes as needed without having to swim through your entire code base** Since this code belongs to someone else, I need to know how to make use of it, to achieve my goals. that is where the documentation comes in. In there I'll find all the functions and features available to me. --- ### Broad overview A good example for this would be a select menu. Every single front-end developer would hate to implement this one in particular over and over especially with the HTML _Select_ tag. that thing is widely known for being an anti-stylalble element 😂 **Vanilla Js** ```html title="custom select-menu markup"

default option

option 1

option 2

option 3

``` > say you had about 4 select elements on a page? you'll repeat ☝ this 5. times. > you could manage it would js programatically. however it's just going to be > more headache **React** ```javascript title="custom select-menu markup" const SelectMenu = ()=> { return (

default option

option 1

option 2

option 3

)} export default SelectMenu; ``` **Rendering** > Now just like the _Vanilla JS_ example. if we need 5 select elements we'll > simply import this into the page as a component. it could become even more > complex with things like _Props_ and _State_. But we'll keep things simple ```javascript title="custom select-menu markup" import SelectMenu from "path-to-component/SelectMenu";
; ``` At this point there are a couple of things the author(s) of the library, has taken care of. which would have been done manually in vanilla js. an example of this is rendering the component to the _DOM_. This is where part of the magic happens. 🧙 - No selecting of elements on the page - No Hairy Computations with the DOM API - No Templates to be created time and again to render your UI's A lot of the manual work of processing before rendering a UI has been taken care of. All you need to focus on is: Composing your UI layout There's a lot more to react especially since it is an unopinonoated library. it can easily integrate with a ton of other things. whilst giving you the liberty to cook however you please | **So long as you don't break the rules** --- ### Conclusion in this article we looked at React from a broad perspective. mainly for the problem it solves. Now that you've gotten the bigger picture. You're set to start digging on your own. Visit the [documentation](https://react.dev/learn) to learn more
